actors.name: Agent
actors.description: AI agent (ga.py) that invokes browser tools: web_execute_js, do_web_scan, do_web_execute_js

actors.name: Browser Extension
actors.description: Chrome MV3 extension (tmwd_cdp_bridge/background.js) that executes JS in tabs and relays results via WebSocket

actors.name: Browser Tab
actors.description: Individual Chrome tab; JS executed via chrome.scripting.executeScript (MAIN world) or CDP fallback

actors.name: Remote TMWebDriver
actors.description: Optional remote instance of TMWebDriver; local instance proxies commands via HTTP /link endpoint

use_cases.name: Initialize Driver
use_cases.description: TMWebDriver.__init__ checks if remote instance exists; if local, starts WS server (port 18765) and HTTP server (port 18766)
use_cases.notes: is_remote flag set by probing port+1; remote mode routes all commands via HTTP /link

use_cases.name: Connect Browser Session
use_cases.description: Extension connects WS to ws://127.0.0.1:18765 and sends ext_ready with all open tabs; JSExecutor.handle() registers each tab as a Session
use_cases.notes: Session tracks id, url, title, type, ws_client/http_queue, connect_at, disconnect_at

use_cases.name: Find / Select Session
use_cases.description: Agent queries available sessions by URL pattern to target a specific browser tab
use_cases.notes: set_session updates default_session_id; remote mode proxies via /link?cmd=find_session

use_cases.name: Execute JavaScript in Browser
use_cases.description: Core use case: agent submits JS code; TMWebDriver dispatches to correct session; browser executes and returns DOM result
dispatch_flow: Browser Tab executes JS in MAIN world
Extension → ws.send({type:result, id, result}) OR ws.send({type:error, ...})
driver → self.results[exec_id] set → execute_js returns {data, newTabs}
use_cases.ack_protocol: Extension sends {type:ack, id} immediately on receipt; driver resets timeout on ACK
use_cases.csp_fallback: If executeScript fails (CSP), extension falls back to Chrome Debugger Protocol (CDP Runtime.evaluate)

use_cases.name: Navigate Browser (Jump / New Tab)
use_cases.description: Agent navigates current tab or opens a new tab via JS execution wrappers
use_cases.notes: jump executes window.location.href=url; newtab executes GM_openInTab(url); both rely on execute_js

use_cases.name: Scan Page HTML
use_cases.description: Agent retrieves and simplifies page HTML for LLM context; optionally lists tabs only
use_cases.notes: HTML is simplified/truncated via BeautifulSoup to reduce token count; tabs_only=true skips HTML fetch

use_cases.name: Monitor Page Changes
use_cases.description: Before/after JS execution, capture DOM diff and transient text strings to report what changed
use_cases.notes: Transient text monitor injects JS polling interval; detects ephemeral UI feedback (toasts, alerts)

use_cases.name: Handle Session Reconnect / Disconnect
use_cases.description: Extension reconnects on page reload; stale sessions are detected and marked; auto-failover to any alive session
use_cases.notes: Sessions inactive >600s are deleted; execute_js auto-switches to alive session if default is disconnected

use_cases.name: Receive Long-Poll Commands (HTTP Mode)
use_cases.description: For environments without WS support, browser tab polls /api/longpoll every 5s; results posted to /api/result
use_cases.notes: HTTP sessions timeout after 60s inactivity; long-poll blocks up to 5s per request

use_cases.name: Execute CDP / Cookie / Batch Commands
use_cases.description: Agent sends structured JSON as JS code; extension routes to handleExtMessage for CDP, cookie, tab management, or batch operations
use_cases.notes: Code payload parsed as JSON object with .cmd field to trigger extension protocol vs raw JS execution

use_cases.name: Proxy Commands to Remote Driver
use_cases.description: When is_remote=True, all commands (execute_js, get_all_sessions, find_session) are forwarded via HTTP POST to remote /link endpoint
use_cases.notes: Enables multi-process or remote-host browser control without running a local WS server

key_relationships: TMWebDriver composes Session (1..*) — one per browser tab
TMWebDriver creates JSExecutor (inner WebSocket handler class) inside start_ws_server
JSExecutor handles WebSocket messages → dispatches to TMWebDriver._register_client / results dict
Browser Extension connects to TMWebDriver WebSocket server
Browser Extension executes JS in Browser Tab via chrome.scripting.executeScript (MAIN world)
Browser Extension falls back to CDP (chrome.debugger) for CSP-restricted pages
simphtml.execute_js_rich extends execute_js with before/after DOM diffing
Remote mode: TMWebDriver delegates to another TMWebDriver instance via HTTP /link
important_notes: Three session transport types: ws (content script WebSocket), ext_ws (Chrome extension manages all tabs via single WS), http (long-polling). Extension sends ext_ready on connect listing all open tabs; tabs_update on tab open/close/navigate. CSP headers stripped by extension declarativeNetRequest rule. exec_id UUID used for request/response correlation. Payload dispatched by session type: ws_client.send_message() or http_queue.put(). Ports: WS=18765, HTTP=18766.
